more.title: East African Community’s expansion has triggered financial troubles: why solutions come with risks
more.description: The East African Community is one of Africa’s oldest regional economic organisations. Its birth in 1967 was the culmination of decades of economic ties forged in the colonial era between Kenya, Uganda and Tanzania. It’s no surprise that the EAC is also the most deeply integrated regional entity. In its heyday between 1967 and 1977, [...]

more.title: The most valuable currency in South Africa’s ride-hailing economy? Trust
more.description: Difficult to earn and remarkably easy to lose, trust has become one of the most important drivers of loyalty in South Africa’s increasingly competitive ride-hailing market. more.title: South Africa raises lemon export forecast as favourable conditions boost crop prospects
more.description: The Citrus Growers’ Association of Southern Africa has revised its lemon export estimates for the 2026 season upwards, citing favourable growing conditions and effective logistical management, despite challenges posed by recent floods.

more.title: South Africans face higher electricity bills as municipal tariff hikes begin
more.description: As winter temperatures drop across South Africa, households are preparing for another increase in monthly expenses as electricity prices rise from July. From 1 July, municipalities across the country will introduce their annual electricity tariff adjustments, placing additional pressure on families already dealing with higher living costs. The latest increases follow Eskom’s own tariff hike for direct customers, which came...
